CREATE TABLE items(item_id integer primary key, name varchar, price decimal);
CREATE TABLE orders(order_id integer primary key, item_id integer references items(item_id), quantity integer);
INSERT INTO items(item_id, name, price) VALUES (1, 'Item A', 10.00);
INSERT INTO items(item_id, name, price) VALUES (2, 'Item B', 20.00);
INSERT INTO items(item_id, name, price) VALUES (3, 'Item C', 15.00);
SELECT item_id, name FROM items WHERE price > (SELECT avg(price) FROM items);
SELECT o.order_id, i.name, o.quantity FROM orders o JOIN items i ON o.item_id = i.item_id WHERE o.quantity > (SELECT count(*) FROM orders WHERE item_id = i.item_id);
CREATE TABLE suppliers(supplier_id integer primary key, name varchar, rating integer);
INSERT INTO suppliers(supplier_id, name, rating) VALUES (1, 'Supplier A', 3);
SELECT i.item_id, s.name FROM items i LEFT JOIN suppliers s ON i.item_id = s.supplier_id WHERE s.rating IS NOT NULL;
INSERT INTO items(item_id, name, price) VALUES (4, 'Item D', 25.00);
SELECT distinct i.name FROM items i JOIN orders o ON i.item_id = o.item_id GROUP BY i.name HAVING COUNT(o.order_id) > 1;
